summary: A Ukrainian Su-24 fighter crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both crew. Russia relocated at least three Tu‑95 strategic bombers from Engels to Olenya airfield. Multiple UAV attacks hit civilian targets in Kherson and Kirovohrad regions.
key_points: A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region during a mission, with both pilots killed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Russia moved at least three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers from Engels to Olenya, with reports of additional two bombers relocating (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).
Russian UAVs attacked a minibus in Kherson, killing one civilian and wounding three, and also struck an ambulance, injuring two medics (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
A UAV strike in the Oleksandriysky district of Kirovohrad region killed over 20 head of cattle (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
Reactive UAVs launched from the Black Sea were observed heading toward Chornomorsk and the city of Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/65345, @kpszsu/65374).
UAVs from Chernihiv region were reported heading toward the Kyiv water reservoir (Source: @kpszsu/65348).
Russian defence ministry brief claimed up to 100 Ukrainian militants were killed in Konstantinovka in a 24‑hour period (Source: @tass_agency/380944).

sections.executive_summary: A Ukrainian Su‑24M fighter crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both crew members. Russia relocated multiple Tu‑95 strategic bombers from Engels to Olenya, indicating a shift in long‑range strike posture. UAV attacks continued across southern and central Ukraine, targeting civilian transport and livestock.

points: Ukrainian Su‑24M aircraft crashed in Khmelnytskyi region while conducting a mission, with both the pilot and navigator confirmed dead; rescue teams reported no civilian casualties at the crash site (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a minibus in Kherson that killed one passenger and injured three others; a separate UAV hit an ambulance, wounding two medics (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
In the Oleksandriysky district of Kirovohrad region, a UAV attack resulted in the death of more than 20 head of cattle, highlighting the impact on agricultural assets (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
Ukrainian authorities observed reactive UAVs originating from the Black Sea heading toward the city of Chornomorsk, indicating continued aerial pressure on southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/65345).

Russian military relocated at least three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers from Engels airfield to Olenya, with preliminary information suggesting two additional bombers may also be moving (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).

The Russian Ministry of Defence brief claimed that up to 100 Ukrainian militants were eliminated in Konstantinovka within a 24‑hour period, emphasizing intensified ground operations (Source: @tass_agency/380944).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the southeastern direction, indicating possible strike preparations (Source: @kpszsu/65349).
Russian sources noted the deployment of reactive UAVs from the Black Sea towards the Chornomorsk direction, suggesting a focus on maritime targets (Source: @kpszsu/65345).
UAV activity was reported in Dnipropetrovsk region, with a drone observed in the Nikopol district moving north‑eastward (Source: @kpszsu/65363).
Another UAV was sighted over Zaporizhzhia approaching the city from the east, indicating continued aerial surveillance in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65380).
A UAV was reported near the town of Apostolove in Dnipropetrovsk, suggesting possible targeting of logistics routes (Source: @eRadarrua/96901).
Ukrainian media claimed Russian forces are using the new "Banderol" drone missile against Kyiv, with a 500 km range and 150 kg warhead (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30935).
A UAV from Chernihiv was observed heading toward the Kyiv water reservoir, raising concerns about critical infrastructure security (Source: @kpszsu/65348).
Additional UAVs were reported along the border near Dobryanka and Slavutych, indicating heightened activity along the northern frontier (Source: @eRadarrua/96922, @eRadarrua/96902).
UAVs were sighted moving from Sumy toward Novhorod‑Siverskyi, suggesting cross‑border reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/65377).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the eastern direction, potentially affecting northern operational zones (Source: @kpszsu/65371).
Reactive UAVs launched from the Black Sea were tracked heading toward Chornomorsk and the city of Chornomorsk, indicating pressure on southern port facilities (Source: @kpszsu/65345, @kpszsu/65374).
UAV attacks in Kherson targeted civilian transport, including a minibus and an ambulance, resulting in casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
A UAV strike in the Olexandriysky district of Kirovohrad region killed over 20 cattle, showing the conflict's impact on southern agricultural zones (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity over the Zaporizhzhia region from the east, potentially monitoring supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/65380).
A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region during a combat mission, with both crew members killed; no civilian injuries were reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the southeastern and eastern directions, suggesting preparation for strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/65349, @kpszsu/65371).
A military aircraft crash was reported in Khmelnytskyi region, with some Ukrainian sources speculating it was a MiG‑29, though details remain unconfirmed (Source: @tass_agency/380970).
Relocation of strategic Tu‑95 bombers from Engels to Olenya indicates a shift in Russia's long‑range aerial capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).

key_developments: Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both pilots (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Russia moved at least three Tu‑95MS strategic bombers from Engels to Olenya airfield (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67824).
UAV attacks in Kherson killed one civilian on a minibus and injured two medics in an ambulance (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
UAV strike in Oleksandriysky district of Kirovohrad region killed over 20 head of cattle (Source: @UkraineNow/81962).
Reactive UAVs from the Black Sea were observed heading toward Chornomorsk and the city of Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/65345).
Russian defence brief claimed up to 100 Ukrainian militants eliminated in Konstantinovka within 24 hours (Source: @tass_agency/380944).
Ukrainian media reported Russian use of the new "Banderol" drone missile against Kyiv with a 500 km range (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30935).
sections.threat_assessment: The crash of a Ukrainian Su‑24M reduces air combat capability in the western sector, while Russia's relocation of Tu‑95 strategic bombers suggests a re‑orientation toward longer‑range strike options. Continued UAV activity across southern and central Ukraine threatens civilian infrastructure and agricultural assets, increasing pressure on logistics and morale. Russian claims of high Ukrainian casualty numbers in Konstantinovka may reflect intensified ground operations in the east, raising the risk of further escalations.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air‑defence and rescue operations in the Khmelnytskyi region following the Su‑24 crash, while local administrations in Kherson and Kirovohrad are coordinating emergency medical and agricultural support after UAV attacks. Russian military logistics are adapting to the new basing of Tu‑95 bombers at Olenya, potentially extending strike reach toward NATO‑adjacent areas. Both sides appear to be consolidating assets in response to evolving aerial threats.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths from the Black Sea and northern border regions is critical for early warning of potential strikes on key infrastructure. Verification of the Tu‑95 relocation should be prioritized to assess changes in Russia's strategic bombing posture. The reported high Ukrainian casualty figures in Konstantinovka require independent confirmation to gauge the true scale of ground engagements.
